info.title: Lulan API
info.description:
Open-source headless reservation engine for transit: segment-aware inventory, race-free booking, itineraries (one-way, round-trip, multi-city), signed quotes, per-passenger Ed25519 QR tickets with offline validation, webhooks, and API-key auth.

Authentication: storefront endpoints (search, quotes, orders) work unauthenticated with guest checkout (`guest_contact` + retrieval token) or with a customer JWT (`Authorization: Bearer <jwt>`) validated against the operator's IdP — JWKS (Auth0, Clerk, Keycloak, Supabase, Firebase, anything publishing a JWK Set) or an HS256 shared secret. Server-to-server endpoints take an API key via `X-Api-Key` — roles: `operator_admin` (webhooks/keys), `integration` (read any order, claim capacity directly, capture payment), `validator` (scan sync). `POST /v1/orders` honours an `Idempotency-Key` header: the first 201 for a key is stored and replayed verbatim on retries.

Admin operations (`/v1/admin/*`) require an enrolled STAFF JWT (roles: admin / ops / support; admin implies all) — or, for staff management only, an operator_admin API key (bootstrap). Every admin mutation is audited with the acting human's staff id.

Browsers: the API sends no CORS headers unless the operator names allowed origins (LULAN_CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS). A storefront calling Lulan directly from the browser needs its origin configured; otherwise route calls through your own backend.

Payments are a port: the running provider is deployment configuration (LULAN_PAYMENT_PROVIDER), described by a JSON file or a built-in preset. Lulan never touches card data and never learns a provider's vocabulary — an adapter normalises callbacks to captured / failed. With no provider configured, the fake provider captures WITHOUT taking money: dev only.

Rate limits apply to reads and writes in separate per-caller buckets, so browsing seat maps cannot exhaust the budget needed to complete a booking. Callers are identified by credential when present, otherwise by connection address; `X-Forwarded-For` is honoured only as far as the operator declares proxies in front (LULAN_TRUSTED_PROXY_HOPS).

post.summary: Validate, publish, and activate a fare ruleset (ops); previous kept for rollback

400.description: Fails the pricing schema or has no base fares

post.summary: Activate a stored ruleset (rollback/switch, ops)
Create a location (ops)

get.summary: Search orders by contact, passenger name, or trip (support)

post.summary: Full refund via the payment port; frees seats, voids tickets (support)
Create a route with per-stop schedule offsets (ops)
Enrol an IdP identity as staff (admin)
Rotate the ticket signing key
post.description:
Mints a new Ed25519 key and makes it active. Takes effect immediately on every replica: issuance reads the active key per ticket rather than caching one at boot.

Previous keys stay published by GET /v1/ticket-keys, because tickets already in passengers' wallets were signed with them and must keep verifying until they expire. Rotation changes what gets signed next; it does not invalidate what was signed before. Responding to a leaked key means rotating AND revoking the tickets it signed.

post.summary: Schedule departures (ops)
Cancel a departure; cascades — unpaid orders cancelled, paid refunded (ops)

200.description: Trip cancelled; counts for what this call settled plus `remaining`

post.description: Cancelling the trip is immediate: it stops being sold the moment this returns. Settling the orders on it happens beyond a first bounded batch in the background, driven off the trip's cancelled status, so a full sailing does not turn one request into hundreds of provider round-trips. `remaining` reports how many orders are still awaiting settlement; a failed refund never releases the seat and is retried.

get.summary: Passenger manifest: seats, passengers, ticket/boarding state (support)

post.summary: Create a vehicle/capacity template (ops)

get.description: Everything the operator sells alongside the fare — baggage, meals, insurance, priority boarding. Add lines to quotes/orders via their codes. No hold required: nothing here is scarce (scarce add-ons are capacity units — extra legroom is a seat, baggage kilos are the cargo pool).

post.summary: Add a catalog entry (admin)

delete.summary: Deactivate a catalog entry (admin; sold orders keep their snapshots)

post.summary: Mint an API key (admin; plaintext returned once)

200.description: The new key — store it now

post.summary: Hold a one-way or round-trip seat selection (one itinerary hold)
post.description:
Soft-holds a one-way or round-trip selection as one itinerary hold. All-or-nothing. Holds are advisory: they protect a selection from other holds while the customer fills in details, and never gate the sale.

Because this endpoint needs no credential, two limits bound how much inventory one session can take off sale: at most 20 seats per request, and a ceiling on how much of a single trip may be held at once (LULAN_HOLD_MAX_TRIP_FRACTION, default 0.75). Hitting the ceiling is a 409 — the booking still completes without a hold.

409.description: A seat is already held or sold; nothing was held. A departure that has left its requested origin, or that ops has cancelled, is refused here too — a trip id bypasses search, so sellability is enforced where inventory resolves, or the trip is at its hold ceiling (book without a hold, or retry shortly).

503.description: Holds unavailable (Redis down); claims still work

post.summary: Create an order (atomic multi-passenger booking)
post.description: Claims every item atomically — any conflict rolls back the whole order. Authentication: either a customer JWT bearer token, or guest checkout with `guest_contact`. Supports `Idempotency-Key` for safe retries. With `quote_token`, quoted prices are honoured; without it, items are priced live.

201.description: Order created, inventory locked (15-minute payment window)

409.description: An item lost the race and nothing was booked — or the presented hold_id has expired / does not cover the itinerary (re-hold or retry without hold_id) — or the Idempotency-Key is in flight, or was already used for a different request. A departure that has left its requested origin, or that ops has cancelled, is refused here too — a trip id bypasses search, so sellability is enforced where inventory resolves.

get.summary: Fetch an order with its event trail
get.description: Gated: pass the retrieval token (`?token=`), the owning customer's bearer JWT, or any API key.

post.summary: Cancel an order (releases claims atomically)
post.description: Gated like the order itself: pass the retrieval token (`?token=`), the owning customer's bearer JWT, or an API key.

409.description: Not cancellable in the current state

post.summary: Attach a guest order to the authenticated customer
Request payment (creates a provider intent)
post.description: Gated like the order itself: pass the retrieval token (`?token=`), the owning customer's bearer JWT, or an API key. The intent id returned here is what captures the payment, so the order id alone does not authorise it. The response also carries `provider` and, when the provider uses one, a `client_secret` for the browser SDK.

post.summary: Issue tickets for a paid order (idempotent retry path)
Payment provider callback (capture / failure)
post.description:
The configured provider's async notification; a capture auto-issues tickets. Idempotent — duplicate and out-of-order deliveries are acknowledged with 200 and never re-applied.

Authentication depends on the provider, because it has to. A provider that signs its callbacks (any real PSP) is authenticated by that signature, verified against the raw body — it cannot be made to send an API key. A provider that does not sign (the built-in fake one) requires an `integration` or `operator_admin` key instead. This endpoint turns an order into a boarding pass; it is never open.

post.summary: Price an itinerary and get a signed short-TTL quote token
post.description: Prices reflect passenger type (regulated concession fares: child, senior, disability), occupancy tiers, peak days, advance purchase, and promos. The returned quote_token locks these prices for checkout (5 minutes). Passenger type is part of the token's match key.

409.description: Conflict. A departure that has left its requested origin, or that ops has cancelled, is refused here too — a trip id bypasses search, so sellability is enforced where inventory resolves.

get.summary: Tickets to refuse despite a valid signature
get.description:
A signature proves a ticket was issued; it cannot prove the ticket is still good, because refunds and trip cancellations happen after signing. Gate devices cache this list next to GET /v1/ticket-keys and pass it to `lulan_validate::verify_ticket_with_revocations`.

With `trip_id` the answer is complete for that departure however far off it is — a gate must never receive a truncated list for the trip it is boarding. Without it, the list covers trips departing in the next 72 hours so it cannot grow without bound.

Coverage is bounded by how recently a device synced. That is the same honest limit clone detection lives with: an offline device cannot know what it has not been told.

parameters.name: trip_id
parameters.description: Scope to one departure; the answer is then complete.

200.description: Revoked ticket ids, with as_of and horizon_hours

post.summary: Sync a device's boarding-scan journal (batched, idempotent)
post.description: Requires a validator or operator_admin API key. Replaying a journal is harmless; duplicate scans across devices are recorded as cloned-QR evidence.

get.summary: Ed25519 public keys for offline ticket validation
get.description: Boarding devices cache this while online; @lulan/validate then verifies tickets with no server dependency.

get.summary: Search a one-way or round-trip itinerary

parameters.name: return_date
parameters.description: Required for round_trip, forbidden for one_way

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Departures per leg. Clamped to 200 — search needs no credential, so its cost is bounded.

get.description: Returns candidate trips per leg (each enriched with operator, service number, vehicle, and schedule). Pick one trip per leg to build a journeys[] quote/order. Multi-city is composed client-side from several one-way searches.

get.summary: Per-seat and per-pool availability for a journey span

200.description: Every seat's availability on the exact span, plus pool remainders

post.summary: Directly claim a span (low-level; prefer POST /v1/orders)
post.description: Requires an `integration` or `operator_admin` key. A claim here sells capacity outside the order lifecycle — no expiry, no payment, no release endpoint — so it is never reachable anonymously. Public booking goes through POST /v1/orders, whose claims are provisional and swept on expiry.

409.description: Conflict. A departure that has left its requested origin, or that ops has cancelled, is refused here too — a trip id bypasses search, so sellability is enforced where inventory resolves.

post.summary: Register a webhook endpoint (admin)
post.description: Deliveries are HMAC-signed: `X-Lulan-Signature: t=<unix>,v1=hex(hmac_sha256(secret, "<t>.<body>"))`. Retries with exponential backoff up to 8 attempts. Empty event_types subscribes to everything.

customerJwt.description: Customer token from the operator's IdP

staffJwt.description: IdP JWT of an ENROLLED staff member (staff table grants the role)

RetrievalToken.name: token
RetrievalToken.description: Guest retrieval token issued at order creation

IdempotencyKey.name: Idempotency-Key
IdempotencyKey.description: Makes a booking retry safe. The key is reserved before the order is created, so concurrent retries cannot both book; the first 201 is then stored and replayed verbatim. Keys are scoped to the caller (customer, or guest contact) and bound to the request body — one client's key never returns another's order, and reusing a key for a different request is refused with 409. A request that books nothing releases its key for reuse. Stored responses are replayable for 24 hours.

Conflict.description: State conflict (raced or wrong lifecycle state)

RateLimited.description: Rate limit exceeded (sliding window per key/IP)

birthdate.description: Optional; lets staff verify age-based fares at boarding

passenger.description: Index into passengers[]. Required for seats unless there is exactly one passenger; forbidden for pools

guest_contact.description: Email or phone; required unless authenticated as a customer

hold_id.description: Itinerary hold from POST /v1/holds, released once the order's claims succeed

ancillaries.description: Must match the quote token's lines verbatim when a token is presented

CreateOrderRequest.description: An itinerary: either journeys[] (round-trip / multi-city) or the single-trip shape trip_id + items (sugar for a one-journey itinerary). Trip type is derived: 1 journey = one-way; 2 journeys with reversed O&D = round-trip (round-trip fare rules apply); otherwise multi-city.

total_minor.description: Sum of item prices, minor units

currency.description: ISO 4217 code, from the fare ruleset that priced the order (or the quote token that locked it)

trip_ids.description: Distinct trips this itinerary touches, in item order

retrieval_token.description: Read credential for this order — deliver to the guest

ancillaries.description: Priced into the total and locked by the quote token

QuoteRequest.description: Same itinerary shapes as order creation: journeys[] or trip_id + items.

HoldRequest.description: Either journeys[] (round-trip / multi-seat across legs) or the single-trip shape trip_id + items.

ttl_seconds.description: Hold lifetime in seconds. Clamped to the operator's window (default 600, min 30, max 1800; configurable via LULAN_HOLD_DEFAULT_TTL_SECS / LULAN_HOLD_MAX_TTL_SECS). Holds always expire and auto-release their seats.

hold_id.description: One id for the whole itinerary hold

held.description: Another session soft-holds an overlapping span right now (advisory; false when holds are unavailable). available reflects sold state only.

token.description: Signed QR payload (LT1.<payload>.<sig>); render as QR client-side

secret.description: Present only in the creation response

arrives_at.description: Arrival at the requested destination (UTC); omitted if no schedule

duration_minutes.description: Journey time for the span; omitted if no schedule

kind.description: Grouping for display (baggage, meal, insurance, service…)

AncillaryLine.description: Same shape at quote and order — the token match is verbatim.

trip_id.description: Required for journey-scoped add-ons: which leg

passenger.description: Required for per-passenger add-ons: index into passengers[]
